skills.skill: Place value models up to thousands
skills.prompt: This topic covers Place value models up to thousands for Grade 3 students (TEKS 3.2(A)). Students use visual and physical models to understand place value. Base-ten blocks, bundles of sticks, and place value charts help students see that digits in different positions represent different values. Key concepts include representing numbers with models, reading models to determine the number shown, and understanding the relationship between ones, tens, and hundreds. Vocabulary: place value, model, base-ten blocks, ones, tens, hundreds, represent, value, digit. Common misconceptions include miscounting blocks, confusing tens rods with ones cubes, and not connecting models to written numbers. Activities should include building numbers with blocks, matching models to numerals, and comparing numbers using models appropriate for ages 8-9.

Activities should include base-ten block building, writing equations, and number form conversion exercises appropriate for ages 8-9.
